metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Major corrections from official rulebook, BGG FAQ, RulePop interactive rulebook.

key_rulings.text: When you Lead: deploy a card to a location (trigger its deploy ability), then collect the TOP card from a DIFFERENT location and gain that location's bonus — OR draw from the deck and roll the Rising die.
When you Scout: reveal the top card of the deck and place (NOT deploy — no ability triggers) it face-up on any location. Gain that location's bonus.
Deploy triggers the card's deploy ability. Place does NOT. Only the Lead action's Step 1 triggers deploy abilities. Scout and die effects use Place.
The Sovereign token is worth 10 VP at game end. Gained by collecting from Luna.
Each Helium token is worth 3 VP at game end.
Influence at The Institute: most tokens = 4 VP each, second most = 2 VP each, all others = 1 VP each.
At game end, lose 10 VP for each card in your hand beyond 7.
The game ends when any single player meets 2 of the 3 conditions, OR when any combination of 3 conditions are met across all players. Conditions: Fleet at 7+, Influence 7+, Helium 7+.
When you collect from Luna: gain/keep the Sovereign token, advance 1 on Fleet, gain 1 Helium, roll the Rising die, and trigger your House ability.
When collecting during Lead, take only the TOP card from the chosen location — not all cards.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **112 character cards** (65×100mm) — Each belongs to one of **14 Colors** (castes) from the Red Rising universe. Every card has: a core value (upper-left number), a deploy ability (middle text — triggers when deployed), and an end-game bonus condition (bottom text — scores if conditions are met at game end).
- **6 reference cards** — Summarize turn actions and end-game triggers.
- **25 Automa cards** — For solo play.

#### Board & Locations
- **1 game board** (410×284mm) — Shows 4 locations: **Jupiter**, **Mars**, **Luna**, and **The Institute**. Each has a card stack area and a bonus icon. Also shows the **Fleet Track** (positions 0-14).

#### Tokens
- **60 Helium tokens** — Red gem-shaped tokens. The game's main resource. Worth **3 VP each** at game end.
- **60 Influence tokens** (10 per player in 6 colors) — Placed at The Institute. Scored at game end via tiered majority.
- **6 Fleet tokens** — One per player, placed on the Fleet Track.
- **1 Sovereign token** — Large ornate token worth **10 VP** at game end. Changes hands throughout the game.
- **1 Rising token** — Crescent moon first-player marker.
- **1 custom Rising die** (22mm) — 6 faces: Jupiter (advance Fleet), Mars (gain Helium), Luna (gain Sovereign), Institute (place Influence), Banish (remove a card), Reveal/Place (deck to board).

#### House Tiles
- **6 House tiles** — Asymmetric starting powers. Each gives a unique ability that triggers whenever you gain the Sovereign token:
  - **Apollo** — First AND last turn of the game. Reveal/place top deck card.
  - **Ceres** — Start with 6 cards. Banish 1 card from a location. Must banish 1 from hand before scoring.
  - **Diana** — Place 1 Influence at The Institute.
  - **Jupiter** — Advance 1 extra on Fleet Track.
  - **Mars** — Gain 1 extra Helium.
  - **Minerva** — Roll the Rising die for an extra bonus.

#### Other
- **1 wolf-head card tray & lid** — Holds the deck.
- **1 scorepad** — For end-game scoring.
- **2 rulebooks** (multiplayer + solo)

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
Red Rising is a **hand-management combo game**. Your hand of cards at the end IS your score — every card has a core value plus conditional end-game bonuses. On each turn, you either **Lead** (deploy a card, then collect a card and a location bonus) or **Scout** (reveal a deck card to a location, gain its bonus). The game ends when enough end-game conditions are triggered across players.

**Critical rule: -10 VP per card beyond 7 in hand.** Your hand can grow, but excess cards are heavily penalized.

#### Setting Up
1. **Shuffle all 112 character cards** into a face-down deck. Place in the card tray.
2. **Place 2 face-up cards** at each of the 4 locations (8 cards total on the board).
3. **Deal 5 cards** to each player as their starting hand (6 for House Ceres).
4. **Each player draws 1 random House tile** and takes their 10 matching Influence tokens.
5. **Place all Fleet tokens** on position 0 of the Fleet Track.
6. **Place Helium supply** near the board.
7. **Place the Sovereign token** beside the board — unclaimed.
8. **Give the Rising token** to House Apollo (if in play) or choose randomly.
9. **Place the Rising die** near the board.

#### Checkpoint
Each player should have: 5 cards in hand (6 for Ceres), 10 Influence tokens in personal supply, a Fleet token on 0, a House tile, and a reference card. The board shows 2 face-up cards at each location. Sovereign and Rising die are nearby.

subtopics.title: Turn Actions: Lead & Scout
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
Do exactly one of two actions: **Lead** or **Scout**.

#### Action 1: Lead (Deploy → Collect)

**Step 1 — Deploy a card.** Choose 1 card from your hand and place it face-up **on top** of any of the 4 locations. This card's **deploy ability** (middle text) triggers now. Deploy abilities can include: draw cards, gain Helium, advance Fleet, place Influence, banish cards, or other effects. You may deploy a card even if its ability conditions aren't met — the ability simply doesn't resolve.

**Step 2 — Collect.** Choose ONE of these options:
- **(a) Collect from a location:** Take the **top card** from a **different** location (NOT the one you just deployed to). Add it to your hand. Then gain that location's bonus.
- **(b) Draw from the deck:** Draw the top card of the deck into your hand. Then **roll the Rising die** and gain the result.

**Location bonuses (gained when collecting from that location):**
- **Jupiter** — Advance your Fleet token **1 space**.
- **Mars** — Gain **1 Helium** from the supply.
- **Luna** — This is the big one. You gain ALL of: the **Sovereign token** (take it, even from another player), **+1 Fleet**, **+1 Helium**, **roll the Rising die**, and **trigger your House tile ability**.
- **The Institute** — Place **1 Influence token** from your supply onto The Institute.

#### Action 2: Scout
1. **Reveal** the top card of the deck.
2. **Place** it face-up on top of any of the 4 locations. (This is a PLACE, not a deploy — the card's deploy ability does **NOT** trigger.)
3. **Gain that location's bonus** (same bonuses as above).

Scout is useful when your hand is already strong and you don't want to give up any card. You still advance on tracks and gain resources.

#### Deploy vs. Place
This is a critical distinction:
- **Deploy** = your card, from your hand, triggers its deploy ability.
- **Place** = a card goes to a location but its ability does NOT trigger.

Only the Lead action deploys. Scout and die effects place.

subtopics.title: Colors, Die & Resources
subtopics.content:
#### The 14 Card Colors (Castes)
Organized by role in the Red Rising universe:

**High Colors (Ruling Class):**
- **Gold** — Highest core values (30-40 avg). Powerful end-game bonuses.
- **Silver** — Helium economy. Exchange Helium for benefits.
- **White** — Sovereign token synergy.
- **Copper** — Institute/Influence synergy.

**Mid Colors (Professional Class):**
- **Blue** — Fleet advancement on deploy.
- **Yellow** — Science/research abilities.
- **Green** — Deck cycling (reveal/place cards from deck to board).
- **Violet** — Creative/artistic themed abilities.
- **Orange** — **Wild for character names.** Can substitute for any named character in end-game bonus conditions.
- **Gray** — **Wild for colors.** Can substitute for any color in end-game bonus conditions.

**Low Colors (Laborer Class):**
- **Brown** — Move cards between locations. Versatile support.
- **Obsidian** — Combat/banishing abilities.
- **Pink** — Move cards between locations.
- **Red** — Low core values but strong set-collection bonuses.

#### The Rising Die
Rolled when you draw from the deck during Lead, and when triggered by Luna. Six faces:
1. **Jupiter** — Advance 1 on Fleet Track.
2. **Mars** — Gain 1 Helium.
3. **Luna** — Gain/keep Sovereign (triggers House ability).
4. **Institute** — Place 1 Influence token.
5. **Banish (X)** — Remove the top card from any location from the game.
6. **Reveal/Place** — Reveal top deck card and place it on any location.

#### Sovereign Token
- Worth **10 VP** at game end.
- Gained by collecting from Luna, rolling Luna on the die, or card abilities.
- When you gain it (even if you already have it), your **House tile ability** triggers.
- Acts as **tiebreaker** — player with Sovereign wins ties.

#### Fleet Track
- Positions 0-14. Scoring is **progressive** and printed on the track (0 VP at position 0, up to **43 VP** at position 14).
- Advance by collecting from Jupiter, deploying Blue cards, rolling Jupiter on die, or collecting from Luna.

#### Influence Tokens
- 10 per player. Placed at The Institute via location bonus, card abilities, or die.
- **Tiered scoring at game end:** Most Influence = **4 VP per token**. Second most = **2 VP per token**. All others = **1 VP per token**.

#### Helium
- Worth **3 VP each** at game end.
- Gained from Mars, Luna, card abilities, or die.
- Some Silver cards convert Helium into other benefits.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Triggers
The game ends when **either**:
- **Any single player** meets **2 of the 3 conditions**, OR
- **Any combination of 3 conditions** are met across all players (by different players).

The three conditions (all at threshold **7**):
1. A player has **7+ Helium tokens**.
2. A player has **7+ Influence tokens** at The Institute.
3. A player's **Fleet token** has reached position **7+** on the Fleet Track.

Once triggered, **all players complete the current round** (equal turns). House Apollo takes the final turn of the game.

**There is NO banish-based trigger.** Only Helium, Influence, and Fleet.

#### Scoring Formula
After end-game abilities resolve:

1. **Card core values** — Sum the upper-left number on every card in your hand.
2. **Card end-game bonuses** — Evaluate each card's bottom text. Score bonuses for met conditions. Some cards penalize if conditions are NOT met.
3. **Fleet Track** — Score VP based on your position (0 at position 0, up to 43 at position 14; progressive scale printed on the track).
4. **Helium** — **3 VP** per Helium token.
5. **Sovereign bonus** — **10 VP** if you hold the Sovereign token.
6. **Influence at The Institute** — Tiered: most = **4 VP/token**, second most = **2 VP/token**, others = **1 VP/token**.
7. **Excess card penalty** — **-10 VP** for each card beyond 7 in your hand.

#### Tiebreaker
Player holding the Sovereign token wins ties.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Core Principle: Hand Quality Over Quantity
Every card scores its core value + potential end-game bonuses. But **-10 VP per card beyond 7** means a bloated hand is devastating. Aim for 5-7 high-synergy cards, not a pile of 10+ mediocre ones.

#### The Scoring Hierarchy
Understand where big points come from:
- **Gold cards** have the highest core values (30-40 range). Even one Gold anchors your score.
- **Fleet Track** can yield up to 43 VP — aggressive Jupiter/Blue strategies are very strong.
- **Sovereign** is a flat 10 VP — don't ignore it.
- **Helium** at 3 VP each adds up fast. 5 Helium = 15 VP.
- **Influence** is tiered — having the MOST tokens makes them worth 4x as much as the bottom tier.

#### Orange and Gray Are the Best Support Colors
- **Orange** cards act as **wild for character names** — they can satisfy end-game bonus conditions that reference specific characters.
- **Gray** cards act as **wild for colors** — they satisfy conditions requiring specific colors.

These make it dramatically easier to complete end-game bonuses on your other cards. Prioritize them.

#### House-Color Synergies
- **House Jupiter + Blues** — Blues advance Fleet on deploy. Jupiter's House ability adds +1 Fleet when gaining Sovereign. Stack for massive Fleet VP.
- **House Diana + Coppers** — Diana places Influence when gaining Sovereign. Coppers synergize with Institute. Aim for Influence majority (4 VP/token).
- **House Mars + Silvers** — Mars gains extra Helium. Silvers convert Helium. Stack Helium for 3 VP each.
- **House Apollo + Whites** — Apollo gets first and last turns. Whites reward holding Sovereign. Secure Sovereign early for 10 VP + White bonuses.

#### Lead vs. Scout
- **Lead** is usually better — you gain a location bonus AND a card, at the cost of deploying one card.
- **Scout** is useful when your hand is already strong (5-6 great cards) and you don't want to risk giving one up. Scout still gives you a location bonus.
- **Scouting to Luna** is powerful — you get Sovereign + Fleet + Helium + die roll + House ability, all without losing a card.

#### Watching End-Game Triggers
- Track everyone's Fleet, Influence, and Helium. The game ends when 1 player hits 2 of 3 triggers, or 3 triggers are met across all players.
- **Don't trigger the end before you're ready.** If your hand is weak, avoid pushing past 7 on any track.
- **If an opponent is close,** either race to finish your combos or deliberately slow down their path.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Holding 8+ cards.** Each card beyond 7 costs you 10 VP. That's more than most cards are worth. Deploy your weakest cards aggressively.
- **Deploying Gold cards.** A 35-point Gold deployed for a Mars bonus (3 VP in Helium) is a 32-VP loss. Deploy your lowest-value cards.
- **Ignoring the Sovereign.** 10 VP is massive. If no one is contesting Luna, Scout there repeatedly.
- **Spreading across all 14 Colors.** Focus on 2-3 Colors that synergize with your House tile. Use Orange/Gray as wild support.
- **Forgetting deploy vs. place.** Scout and die effects PLACE cards — no deploy abilities trigger. Only the Lead action's Step 1 deploys.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Red Rising

**After Setup**: You have 5 cards, 10 Influence tokens, Fleet at 0, a House tile, and no Helium. Each location has 2 face-up cards. The Sovereign is unclaimed.

**Turn 1 — Lead**: Find your lowest-value card. Deploy it to Luna. Its deploy ability triggers (resolve it). Now collect from a different location — say Mars has a Gold card you want. Take the top card from Mars into your hand and gain 1 Helium.

**Turn 2 — Scout to Luna**: You don't want to give up any cards yet. Reveal the top deck card and place it on Luna (no deploy ability triggers). Gain Luna's bonus: take/keep Sovereign (10 VP!), +1 Fleet, +1 Helium, roll the Rising die, trigger your House ability.

**Turns 3-5 — Build combos**: Deploy weak cards, collect strong ones from locations. Look for Orange/Gray cards to act as wilds for your end-game conditions. Watch your hand size — stay at or below 7 cards.

**Mid-Game**: Check the board. Which end-game conditions are approaching 7? If you're at Fleet 5, two more Jupiter collections trigger it. If your hand has strong combos, you WANT to trigger the end. If not, slow down.

**Late Game — Optimize**: Stop deploying valuable cards. Scout if your hand is good. Make sure your end-game bonus conditions are met. Check: do your Orange/Gray wilds complete any conditions?

**End-Game Trigger**: Someone hits 2 of 3 conditions (Fleet 7, Influence 7, Helium 7), or 3 total conditions are met. Everyone finishes the round.

**Scoring**: Use the scorepad. Add: card core values + end-game bonuses + Fleet Track VP + (Helium × 3) + Sovereign (10) + Influence (tiered: 4/2/1 per token). Subtract: 10 per card beyond 7. First-game target: 80-120 is competitive.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Hand Sculpting

Red Rising is fundamentally about building the perfect hand. Every deploy-and-draw decision should ask: 'Does this improve my end-game hand?' A powerful card on the board is only useful if you can retrieve it before the game ends.

#### Color Synergies

**Gold** cards score for set collection. **Silver** cards reward specific board positions. **Obsidian** cards score for combat (helium). **Blue** cards score for influence. **Red** cards are wildcards. Build your hand around 1–2 color synergies, not rainbow collection.

#### Location Mastery

Each location (Jupiter, Mars, Luna, The Institute) offers different card pools and bonuses. **Jupiter** is best for Gold/Silver. **Mars** for Obsidian/combat. **Luna** for influence. **The Institute** for flexibility. Specialize based on your hand direction.

#### Helium Timing

Helium advances you toward Sovereign and triggers the end game. Accelerate when you have a strong hand; stall when you need more sculpting time. Watch opponents' helium — don't let someone end the game when you're mid-build.

#### The Sovereign Bonus

Reaching Sovereign is worth significant points but costs resources. Only race to Sovereign if your hand already scores well — a 60-point hand beats a 40-point hand with the Sovereign bonus.
